title: Sage and the Magic Rainbow Bridge
synopsis: When young Sage's magical rainbow bridge disappears, leaving his fairy friends stranded on the other side of the river, he must overcome his scared feelings and find the courage to help them. With determination and bravery, Sage discovers that friendship and kindness can work magic.

pages.text: Sage loved playing with his fairy friends by the sparkling river. Every morning, a beautiful rainbow bridge appeared so they could visit each other. But today, something was wrong - the bridge was gone!

illustration.description: A small Black boy with curly hair named Sage stands by a glittering blue river, looking confused and worried. Across the river, three diverse fairy friends wave sadly - one Asian fairy with purple wings, one Latina fairy with golden wings, and one white fairy with green wings. The sky is bright blue with fluffy white clouds, and flowers dot the grassy banks.
illustration.alt: Sage looking worried by a river with fairy friends on the other side

pages.text: Sage felt scared and sad. His fairy friends looked scared too. They called across the water, 'We can't get to you, Sage!' Luna the purple fairy looked ready to cry. Sage's heart felt heavy.

illustration.description: Close-up of Sage's expressive face showing worried, sad eyes and a frown. In the background, the three fairy friends hover anxiously on the far riverbank, their wings drooping with sadness. The scene uses soft blues and purples to show the sad mood, with gentle lighting.
illustration.alt: Sage and the fairies all looking sad and worried

pages.text: Sage took deep breaths and felt brave. 'I will help you!' he called. He gathered smooth stones and colorful flowers. Then he whispered magic words his grandmother taught him: 'Friendship strong, bridge come back!'

illustration.description: Sage kneeling by the riverbank with determination on his face, collecting smooth gray stones and bright red, yellow, and blue flowers. His expression shows courage and concentration. Golden sparkles begin to appear around his hands, suggesting magic is starting to work. The background shows a sunny meadow with textured grass.
illustration.alt: Sage bravely collecting stones and flowers with magical sparkles around him

pages.text: Magic sparkles danced in the air! A new rainbow bridge appeared, more beautiful than before. The fairies flew across, happy and excited. They hugged Sage tight. 'You were so brave!' said Luna. Sage felt proud and happy.

illustration.description: A magnificent rainbow bridge arcs across the sparkling river with swirls of magical glitter. Sage stands with his arms outstretched, smiling widely as the three fairy friends fly toward him with joyful expressions. Everyone looks happy and excited, with bright rainbow colors filling the scene and golden sparkles everywhere.
illustration.alt: Sage and his fairy friends reunited on the magical rainbow bridge, all smiling happily

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Sage feel when the rainbow bridge disappeared?
questions.explanation: Sage felt scared and sad when he couldn't reach his fairy friends.

questions.question: What did Sage collect to help make the bridge?
questions.explanation: Sage gathered smooth stones and colorful flowers to help create the magical bridge.

questions.question: How did Sage feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Sage felt proud and happy after he bravely helped his friends and they were reunited.

questions.question: What did the fairy friends say about Sage?
questions.explanation: Luna told Sage 'You were so brave!' because he helped them when he was scared.

pages.text: Sage felt _____ when the bridge was gone, but then he became _____ and helped his friends.
Sage collected _____ and _____ to make magic happen.
The fairy friends were _____ when they could cross the new rainbow _____.
